entries.title: What Is Life? The Physical Aspect of the Living Cell
entries.local_pdf_edition: Digital reprint (social-ecology.org), 32-page reformat — does NOT carry original CUP pagination

verbatim_text_verified.location: Chapter 6, section 'IT FEEDS ON NEGATIVE ENTROPY'
verbatim_text_verified.text: What an organism feeds upon is negative entropy.
verbatim_text_verified.also_verified: We said before: 'It feeds upon negative entropy', attracting, as it were, a stream of negative entropy upon itself, to compensate the entropy increase it produces by living and thus to maintain itself on a stationary and fairly low entropy level.

entries.pagination_note: Citation 'p. 76' refers to Cambridge University Press original edition. Not verifiable from available 32-page digital reprint. The section 'IT FEEDS ON NEGATIVE ENTROPY' falls in PDF segment 24-25 of the reprint. Original CUP edition has ~91 pages; p. 76 is plausible but not locally confirmed.
entries.thesis_relevance: Fundamento para 05-aplicaciones/02-biologia-y-ecologia.md §0.1 cond. 3: metabolismo como intercambio de entropía negativa — negentropía como propiedad termodinámica de sistemas vivos

entries.title: The Statistical Crisis in Science
entries.subtitle: Data-dependent analysis — a 'garden of forking paths' — explains why many statistically significant comparisons don't hold up
entries.license: Author's personal copy hosted on author's Columbia University page (stat.columbia.edu/~gelman/)
entries.pdf_note: High-res scan of journal article. Text extractable via pdftotext. Last page verifies p. 465 and Nov-Dec 2014.
entries.abstract_verified: Reports growing realization that statistically significant claims in scientific publications are routinely mistaken; introduces 'garden of forking paths' concept showing how multiple implicit analytical choices inflate false positives even without intentional p-hacking.
entries.key_concept: Garden of forking paths: researcher degrees of freedom in analytical choices that inflate false positive rates without explicit multiple comparisons
entries.thesis_relevance: Audita la objeción B del adversarial iter 4 (PASS 72): la cita sobre 'forking paths' era secundaria; este PDF permite verificarla contra fuente primaria y establecer páginas exactas para la citación

entries.title: Does Ontology Rest on a Mistake?
entries.license: Author's personal copy hosted on MIT personal page (mit.edu/~yablo/)
entries.local_path: 07-bibliografia/Yablo - Does Ontology Rest on a Mistake (1998).pdf
entries.pdf_note: 148 KB, clean text PDF from author's MIT page. pdftotext confirms full text starts 'DOES ONTOLOGY REST ON A MISTAKE? Stephen Yablo and Andre Gallois'.
entries.abstract_verified: Defends a version of Carnap's internal/external distinction freed from the analytic/synthetic distinction, reframed as the distinction between statements made within make-believe games and those made outside them (metaphorical/literal distinction). Argues that to determine ontological commitments we must ferret out all traces of non-literality; if no sensible project of doing that exists, there is no sensible project of Quinean ontology.
entries.companion_paper: Andre Gallois response in same volume: DOI 10.1111/1467-8349.00045
entries.thesis_relevance: PASS 72 engagement-yablo: fundamento primario para defender que afirmaciones sobre estructuras pre-ontológicas son figural/internal (no comprometidas ontológicamente en sentido quiniano); permite auditar y refinar el capítulo de engagement con Yablo
